task: I’ve got a subcontractor dispute brewing over who’s on the hook for a damaged section, and I want to handle it the right way before it turns into a bigger headache. What should I gather and put in writing first so I’m not boxed in later?

turns.agent_response:
Gather and write down these first:

- **What was damaged**
  - exact location
  - extent of damage
  - what condition it was in before

- **Who was working there**
  - subcontractor names
  - crew members
  - foreman/supervisor
  - equipment on site

- **When it happened**
  - date and time
  - sequence of work leading up to it
  - who was present

- **What each party says happened**
  - keep it factual
  - don’t argue the blame in the write-up

- **Photos and video**
  - before / during / after if you have them
  - wide shot and close-up
  - include reference points

- **Documents**
  - contract scope
  - drawings
  - daily reports
  - delivery tickets
  - inspection notes
  - RFIs / change orders if relevant

- **Immediate actions taken**
  - stopped work
  - barricaded area
  - temporary repair
  - notification sent

### Put in writing
- date/time discovered
- exact damage description
- who was notified and when
- what work was paused
- what evidence was collected
- who is responsible for next review

### Key rule
Do **not** assign blame in the first write-up unless it’s obvious and documented. Just lock down the facts.

### Bring in the right people
If it may affect:
- cost
- schedule
- warranty
- safety
- structure
- insurance

then loop in the super/PM/QC and, if needed, the insurer or owner rep the same day.

If you want, I can give you a **short damage-dispute log template** you can use on site.

user_simulator.text: Close, but I want it tighter. Give me a simple site-ready log template and a clean incident memo format I can hand to a foreman or PM. Keep it practical — fields, not a lecture. And include a line for who had control of the area when it happened. That’s the part people try to dodge.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are both in a clean, site-ready format.

## 1) Simple site log template

**Damage / Dispute Log**

- **Job name:**  
- **Date:**  
- **Time discovered:**  
- **Exact location:**  
- **Area under control of:**  
- **Subcontractor / crew on site:**  
- **Foreman / supervisor:**  
- **Equipment involved:**  
- **What was damaged:**  
- **Condition before damage:**  
- **What happened:**  
- **Sequence of events:**  
- **Witnesses:**  
- **Photos / video taken:** yes / no  
- **Documents attached:** drawings / daily report / delivery ticket / inspection note / RFI / CO / other  
- **Work stopped or paused:** yes / no  
- **Who was notified:**  
- **Time notified:**  
- **Temporary action taken:**  
- **Next review / owner:**  
- **Notes:**  

## 2) Clean incident memo format

**Incident Memo**

**Project:**  
**Date:**  
**Prepared by:**  
**To:** Foreman / PM / Superintendent / QC

### Subject
Damage / dispute involving: **[area / item]**

### Facts
On **[date]** at approximately **[time]**, damage was discovered at **[location]**.  
The area was under control of **[person / subcontractor / crew]** at the time.

### What was observed
- **What was damaged:**  
- **Extent of damage:**  
- **Condition before incident:**  
- **Sequence leading up to it:**  
- **Equipment involved:**  

### Documentation
- Photos/video: yes / no  
- Witnesses:  
- Relevant documents:  

### Action taken
- Work stopped: yes / no  
- Area secured: yes / no  
- Temporary repair / protection:  
- Notifications made:  

### Open items
-  
-  

### Current status
-  
-  

If you want, I can also turn this into a **one-page fill-in form** you can copy into a notebook or text to the crew.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the shape of it. Clean enough to use.

**Damage / Dispute Log**
- Project:
- Date:
- Time discovered:
- Exact location:
- Area under control of:
- Subcontractor / crew on site:
- Foreman / supervisor:
- Equipment involved:
- What was damaged:
- Condition before damage:
- What happened:
- Sequence of events:
- Witnesses:
- Photos / video taken:
- Documents attached:
- Work stopped or paused:
- Who was notified:
- Time notified:
- Temporary action taken:
- Next review / owner:
- Notes:

**Incident Memo**
- Project:
- Date:
- Prepared by:
- To:
- Subject:
- Facts:
- What was observed:
- Documentation:
- Action taken:
- Open items:
- Current status:

And yeah, the **area under control of** line belongs right up near the top. That’s the one people will try to gloss over.

This is usable. No fluff.
